# Copilot Instructions for Route

## Project Overview

Route is a project in active development. This file provides guidance to GitHub Copilot for generating contextually relevant code and suggestions.

## Contribution Guidelines

- Follow best practices for the language and framework being used
- Write clear, maintainable, and well-documented code
- Ensure code is properly formatted before committing
- Include appropriate error handling and validation
- Write descriptive commit messages

## Code Quality Standards

- Use clear and descriptive variable and function names
- Add comments for complex logic or non-obvious implementations
- Keep functions focused and modular
- Follow DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles
- Prioritize code readability and maintainability

## Testing

- Write tests for new features and bug fixes
- Ensure existing tests pass before committing
- Aim for good test coverage of critical functionality

## Documentation

- Update README.md when adding new features or making significant changes
- Document public APIs and interfaces
- Include usage examples where appropriate

## General Preferences

- Prefer clear, explicit code over clever solutions
- Use modern language features appropriately
- Handle edge cases and errors gracefully
- Consider performance implications for critical paths
